preamble: The transformation of the voiced velar plosive ([g]) into the voiced labiodental fricative ([v]) is a documented phonetic shift, primarily occurring through a process of lenition. This change involves both a weakening of the consonant's articulation from a complete stop to a fricative, and a significant shift in its place of articulation from the velar region (back of the tongue against the soft palate) to the labiodental region (lower lip against upper teeth).

examples.note: Pronunciation of 'кого' (kogo) as [kavó] in genitive/accusative case endings.
Pronunciation of 'белого' (belogo) as [bʲeləvə] in genitive/accusative case endings.
